suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The runtime-provider-worker skill requires a failing characterization/regression test first, but this worker explicitly skipped an isolated red-first cycle and implemented tests plus code together. The procedure may need to acknowledge when tightly scoped route changes are reviewed from existing characterization instead of a separate failing-first command.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature exchange-volume-accumulation-and-range: Handoff skillFeedback marks followedProcedure=false for step 2, and worker-transcripts.jsonl for session 2cb5cc39-e01b-4e4c-85ee-e3ea4243bcce shows ApplyPatch edits landed before any successful targeted test run.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The runtime-provider-worker skill says to run the targeted validation set, but assigned validator commands can still fail for unrelated repo-wide drift. The skill should explicitly bless narrower feature-focused confirmation after the required command surfaces unrelated blockers.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature exchange-volume-accumulation-and-range: Handoff skillFeedback includes a suggested skill change after the required test command timed out and exposed unrelated tests/app.test.ts token/bootstrap failures outside exchange-volume scope.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: The compatibility-hardening worker skill still prescribes running the full `bun run test` baseline even for tiny validator-surface alignment tasks, but this feature appropriately proceeded after documenting an unrelated pre-existing timeout because the assigned behavior was limited to manifest/script alignment.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature lint-script-manifest-alignment: compatibility-hardening-worker SKILL.md step 8 requires `bun run test`; handoff skillFeedback marks `followedProcedure: false` and explains the feature only needed `bun run lint` plus `bun run typecheck` after a pre-existing timeout in `tests/invalid-params.test.ts`.
